zh.Kildonan, Kilmory, North Ayrshire, Ayrsh: 英國蘇格蘭諾斯 艾斯爾吉爾莫利
zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Eyckles 2nd Laird of Kildonan於1296年出生在英國蘇格蘭諾斯 艾斯爾吉爾莫利時，他的父親John De Eccles 1st Laird of Kildonan42歲，母親Baroness Oures De Kincardshire32歲。他和Beatrix de Cathcart至少有1個兒子。他去世於1338年之前在英國蘇格蘭。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Eyckles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Eccles  . This form is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Eccles
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Eccles in Lancashire, or possibly from any of several other places so named in Kent, Norfolk, Berwickshire, and Dumfriesshire, from British eglēs ‘Romano-British Christian church’ (from Latin ecclesia, Greek ekklēsia ‘gathering, assembly’). Such places would have been the sites of notable pre-Anglo-Saxon churches or Christian places of communal worship.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Eyckles 2nd Laird of Kildonan was born in 1296, in Kilmory, North Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, John De Eccles 1st Laird of Kildonan, was 42 and his mother, Baroness Oures De Kincardshire, was 32. He had at least 1 son with Beatrix de Cathcart. He died before 1338, in Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William d’Eyckles 2nd Laird of Kildonan est né en 1296, à Kilmory, North Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, John De Eccles 1st Laird of Kildonan, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Baroness Oures De Kincardshire, avait 32 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Beatrix de Cathcart. Il est décédé avant 1338, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de Eyckles 2nd Laird of Kildonan 1296 in Kilmory, North Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John De Eccles 1st Laird of Kildonan 42 und seine Mutter Baroness Oures De Kincardshire 32. Er hatte mit Beatrix de Cathcart mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb vor 1338 in Schottland, United Kingdom.

Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Beatrix de Cathcart. Morì prima del 1338, nel Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.Kildonan, Kilmory, North Ayrshire, Ayrsh: イギリススコットランドノース エイルシュアイアキルモーイ
ja.lifeSummary: William de Eyckles 2nd Laird of Kildonanが1296年にイギリススコットランドノース エイルシュアイアキルモーイで生まれた時，父John De Eccles 1st Laird of Kildonanは42歳，母Baroness Oures De Kincardshireは32歳であった。彼はBeatrix de Cathcartとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1338年以前にイギリススコットランドで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Eyckles 2nd Laird of Kildonan(이)가 1296년 영국 스코틀랜드 노르스 에이르실르 킬모리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John De Eccles 1st Laird of Kildonan(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Baroness Oures De Kincardshire(은)는 32세였다. 그는 Beatrix de Cathcart(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1338년 이전 영국 스코틀랜드에서 사망했다.
